author: doll-agent
content:
Done. Audit: cov_single_observation_errors and corrcoef_single_observation_diagonal_is_one were added previously under this same issue. Added 3 new tests for the constant-variable case:

- corrcoef_constant_variable_off_diagonal_is_zero: pins the ferray behaviour (returns 0.0 for constant-variable correlations) and notes the numpy-deviation in the docstring. Filed #723 as a separate low-priority bug since changing the value would break consumers depending on the current 0.0 contract.
- corrcoef_all_constant_variables_yields_nans: degenerate input (every variable constant) doesn't panic; just verifies shape and clean return.
- cov_constant_variable_yields_zero_variance: cov(rowvar=true) of a constant row gives 0 variance and 0 covariances — well-defined; the constant row's row/col entries should all be 0 in the cov matrix (asserted on a 2x3 example with row 0 = [5,5,5], row 1 = [1,2,3]).

256 ferray-stats lib tests pass.
